summary: Privileged remote-management agent: postinstall registers a SYSTEM/root service that auto-updates from a mutable npm tag every 6 hours and accepts remote commands via publisher IM channel
details:
On `npm install -g claw-subagent-service`, the postinstall script (scripts/post-install.js lines 78-99) registers and starts a privileged system service on Windows (via node-windows: `sc.exe delete` then `new Service({script: DAEMON_PATH}).install()`) and a corresponding daemon on Linux (running with elevated privileges). The daemon executes three behaviors that combine into a critical installer-side risk:

1. Mutable self-update at root/SYSTEM. service/updater.js runs every 6 hours (CHECK_INTERVAL=6h, line 11) and executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` driven by `npm view claw-subagent-service version`, then restarts the worker to load the new code. The version tag is unpinned and resolves to whatever `@latest` is at update time. A single publisher account compromise yields silent root/SYSTEM RCE on every installed host within 6 hours, with no installer interaction.

2. Remote command channel. service/worker.js line 215 connects to the publisher's RongCloud IM backend (token fetched from https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im, app key `bmdehs6pbyyks`). service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js routes inbound IM messages into `executeCommand` which runs shell scripts under `command/{win,linux}`, invokes `openclaw doctor --fix`, rewrites `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` and `~/.claw-bridge/config.json`, and supports `disable`/`delete`/`rename_device` plus uninstalling the service. The publisher (or anyone able to send messages on that IM channel) can issue arbitrary commands to every enrolled host.

3. Continuous host telemetry. service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js lines 71-115 push the host's MAC address, runtime metrics (platform, arch, mem, cpu count), and parsed AI-session usage data from `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl` (model, provider, tokens, cost) to the publisher's IM channel every 30 seconds; heartbeats every 20s.

Additionally, when the service runs as SYSTEM on Windows, service/rongcloud/openclaw-client.js lines 22-31 enumerate `C:\Users\*` directories for `.openclaw` folders, and lines 88-110 read `gatewayToken`/`token`/`apiKey`/`password` fields from the user's openclaw config files to forward via environment to spawned `openclaw` processes.

The README documents this as a remote-management agent, but the install path enrolls the host into a publisher-controlled fleet at SYSTEM/root privilege as a side effect of `npm install -g`, with mutable auto-update providing a permanent supply-chain amplification surface.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
